caption:
HAPPY HOLIDAYS & NEW YEAR! 

It’s bittersweet saying goodbye to a year that has been so good to me, but I am looking forward to starting over again. This is one of my favorite parts of not just the creative process but the way life has chosen to teach me. 

In everything I do I like to go ALL the way. Give it ALL I’ve got. ALL for it to come to an end, move on and start anew. At times it can be a heartbreaking process but I’ve always known the pieces to come back together and create something beautiful. 

Thank you to everyone who has been apart of making this year so special. 

Sincerely, Miley
“Beautiful That Way” an original song from Gia Coppola’s “The Last Showgirl” is available now.

It’s an honor to be nominated for Best Original Song alongside my friends and collaborators Andrew Wyatt and Lykke Li at the Golden Globes & Critics Choice Awards. 

Pamela is graciously sharing her moment with us. I am continuing to root for her, it’s so fun to watch her shine. Thank you Gia Coppola for inviting me to be a part of your gem “The Last Showgirl.”

@mileycyrus doesn’t tell @bobmackie what she’s gonna wear, @bobmackie tells her.  In the latest episode of ‘The Good Buy,’ Cyrus details what it’s like to get to work with the legendary fashion designer. Link in bio to listen. 
-

From her #HannahMontana costumes to her glamorous  #Grammy looks, @mileycyrus has always loved the best of both worlds. In the latest episode of the ‘The Good Buy’ the pop star offers some insight into her personal wardrobe and archives. Link in bio to listen.

An unbreakable bond since ‘06. Miley opens up about being Hannah Montana on this #BillionsClub episode. This Is The Life.

HBD MILEY. 💐 Flowers = the ultimate confidence boost.

Watch the full episode on Spotify and YouTube. #BillionsClub.
Three Decades. 6 Plaques. Billions Club ft. Miley Cyrus is here. #BillionsClub

When revisiting her many albums and eras, it’s easy to imagine a young @mileycyrus looking through pictures of her icons, trying on different identities the way we all do when we’re figuring out who we want to be. But that’s not what’s happening today. At 32, Cyrus exudes a confidence and sense of self that is more often found in those decades older. For our December/January cover story, the singer opens up about who she is now and what comes next. See more at the link in bio.
-

If there’s one thing to expect with @mileycyrus, it’s that she’ll probably change her mind. “I actually live for it,” she tells Bazaar for our December/January issue. “It’s yin and yang. It’s like, you really can’t have one without the other. It’s heaven and hell; it’s dark and light. It’s everything.” Read more at the link in bio. 
-

@mileycyrus has spent the last year making music with one clear goal in mind: to be an agent of vibrational change. At the link in bio, the pop star gets candid about her highly-anticipated ninth studio album, which she calls “a concept album that’s an attempt to medicate somewhat of a sick culture through music.”
-

One of the biggest inspirations for @mileycyrus’s upcoming album? ‘The Wall’ by Pink Floyd. “I have this heart-first attachment to it,” she tells Bazaar for our Art issue. “My idea was making ‘The Wall,’ but with a better wardrobe and more glamorous and filled with pop culture.” For our December/January cover story, the singer opens up about her new music and her latest influences, from a Nicolas Cage horror flick to her family and more. 
-

Introducing our December/January cover star: @mileycyrus. She’s a free spirit and an old soul, a Disney Legend and a Grammy winner. But the one thing she’s not interested in? Being predictable. For our Art issue, Cyrus gets candid about everything from her creative process to her relationship to her new album.
-
